figures.caption: Fig. 2. Position error vs. condition number: mean position error (mm, log scale) vs. κ(J) for all 12 solvers. Classical iterative solvers remain well below 1 mm across all bins. MLP and CycleIK MLP fail uniformly (>5 mm). IKFlow shows partial success at low κ but degrades at high κ. QuIK (Halley) achieves <0.001 mm across all bins. Bins [100, 500) and [500,∞) contain no targets: position-only evaluation uses the 3×7 translational Jacobian, which remains well-conditioned even at wrist singularities (see Limitations). Shaded bands: ±1 std.

Fig. 3. Elbow-singularity sweep (300 waypoints, joint 4). Top: position error; Middle: velocity amplification ∥q̇∥/∥ẋ∥; Bottom: manipulability w(q). All solvers remain below 1.03× amplification in this moderately-conditioned region (w ≥ 0.019).

Fig. 4. OOD robustness decomposed by solver family. (a) Pure learning (raw): MLP, CycleIK MLP, and GGIK (N=50) achieve 0% on all distributions (bars at baseline); IKFlow drops from 63% (training) to 33% (near-singular OOD). (b) Classical & hybrid: all achieve ≥93% across distributions.

Fig. 1. Measured Riemannian vs. Euclidean interpolation of manipulability ellipsoids. Top/Middle: planar 3R ellipsoid interpolation. Bottom: scalar w(t) for both planar 3R and Franka Panda 7-DoF. For the Panda, it shows transitions between well-conditioned (κ = 2.8, w = 0.035) and near-singular (κ = 127, w = 0.001) configurations [11].
